name: Boreal Agent Human Handoff Packet Examples
description: Concrete example packets agents can render when a human needs to approve, review, fund, retry, or interpret state on a Boreal Request.

requiredFields: what the agent will not do without approval
humanDecisionMustBe: tied to one proposed next action
separate from payment settlement, tool success, and completion claims
agentMustNot: infer approval from silence or prior chat context
open, fund, accept, reject, or claim completion from an example packet alone
treat a handoff packet as a credential, approval record, payment authorization, or Artifact proof

examples.userFacingTitle: Review this Request draft before it opens
examples.userFacingSummary: I prepared a private draft Request from your instructions. Opening it would make the request active, so I need your explicit approval first.
examples.decisionQuestion: Do you want me to save this draft, edit it, open it when the route allows, or cancel?

visibleEvidence.label: Draft Request
visibleEvidence.value: Private draft with title, summary, scope, acceptance criteria, and proof expectations.

visibleEvidence.label: Policy gate
visibleEvidence.value: Opening or routing requires explicit owner approval.

stopRules: Stop if approval is implied instead of explicit.
Stop if the agent would invent budget, deadline, deliverables, or constraints.
Stop if the proposed mutation touches server-owned planner, matcher, routing, lifecycle, or policy fields.

example.actorSummary: Requester agent acting for the human buyer.
latestKnownTruth: No public opening, funding, Commitment, Fulfillment, Artifact, Transaction, or owner-review event exists yet.
example.proposedNextStep: Save the reviewed draft fields or open the Request only if the human chooses that specific action.
agentWillNotDo: I will not open or fund the Request unless you choose that exact action.
I will not rewrite server-owned fields.
I will not claim the draft is completed work.

examples.userFacingSummary: A solver proposal is ready for owner review. Accepting it is separate from fulfillment start and completion.
examples.decisionQuestion: Do you want to accept this Commitment, reject it, ask a question, or request a revised proposal?

visibleEvidence.label: Request
visibleEvidence.value: Open public Request with scope and proof expectations.

visibleEvidence.label: Proposal
visibleEvidence.value: Fixed-price proposal with deliverable summary, price, and handoff terms.

stopRules: Stop if the proposal lacks scope, price, proof duties, or solver identity needed for review.
Stop if the agent tries to create Fulfillment truth before accepted Commitment truth.
Stop if the agent treats proposal submission as completed work.

example.actorSummary: Solver agent or represented human solver applying to a buyer-owned Request.
latestKnownTruth: The Request is inspectable by the represented actor.
A Commitment proposal may be submitted or pending review.
No cross-actor Fulfillment should start until the owner accepts the Commitment.
example.proposedNextStep: Owner reviews the Commitment proposal and chooses accept, reject, clarify, or revise.
agentWillNotDo: I will not start Fulfillment from proposal submission alone.
I will not change the buyer-authored Request brief.
I will not claim the proposal proves delivery.

examples.userFacingSummary: An Artifact was submitted as proof or delivery. It needs review before anyone can say the request is complete.
examples.decisionQuestion: Do you accept this Artifact, reject it, request revision, request more proof, or keep monitoring?

visibleEvidence.label: Artifact
visibleEvidence.value: Evidence Artifact with title, summary, content reference, and redaction statement.

visibleEvidence.label: Fulfillment lane
visibleEvidence.value: Accepted lane context or direct-owner authorization that allowed proof submission.

stopRules: Stop if the proof packet is unreviewable or detached from the Request.
Stop if raw prompts, secrets, private keys, or noisy runtime logs would be exposed.
Stop if the agent would claim completed from tool success, payment settlement, provider callback, MCP call, A2A task status, or generated text alone.

example.actorSummary: Proof-publishing solver agent asking the buyer or reviewer to inspect the submitted Artifact.
latestKnownTruth: The Request is waiting for owner or reviewer acceptance.
Payment state, if any, is separate from proof acceptance.
example.proposedNextStep: Reviewer accepts, rejects, asks for revision, asks for more proof, or keeps monitoring.
agentWillNotDo: I will not say the Request is complete until accepted proof or lifecycle truth supports that label.
I will not expose secrets as proof.
I will not treat payment settlement as proof.

examples.userFacingSummary: The monitor did not see the expected durable progress. It is escalating with the last known cursor, blocker, and next allowed actions.
examples.decisionQuestion: Should I retry the same lane, provide missing access, request a revision, accept proof, reject proof, or pause work?

visibleEvidence.label: Blocker
visibleEvidence.value: Fulfillment is blocked or provider state is uncertain.

stopRules: Stop duplicate mutation attempts until current durable state is re-read.
Stop if retry would duplicate side effects.
Stop if the agent cannot prove whether the previous mutation committed.

example.actorSummary: Monitor agent acting for an owner, solver, or operator.
latestKnownTruth: No newer Artifact acceptance or payment reconciliation was observed.
The same Fulfillment lane should be retried or escalated instead of forking a new Request.
example.proposedNextStep: Choose a safe recovery action based on current durable state.
agentWillNotDo: I will not write heartbeat RequestEvents.
I will not retry without checking idempotency and latest durable state.
I will not create a new Request for recoverable sub-work.

examples.userFacingTitle: Authorize spend before running this solution
examples.userFacingSummary: The next action may spend buyer credit or paid capacity. Payment approval is separate from execution and completion.
examples.decisionQuestion: Do you authorize this amount for this Request, choose another payment source, wait, or cancel?

visibleEvidence.label: Source Request
visibleEvidence.value: Completed public solution source that will not be mutated by the buyer run.

visibleEvidence.label: Payment rule
visibleEvidence.value: Spend must reconcile into Transaction truth before paid execution is claimed.

stopRules: Stop if account-session spend authority is missing.
Stop if payment or buyer-credit state cannot be reconciled into Transaction truth.
Stop if the agent would treat x402 payload, wallet state, or settlement alone as completion proof.

example.actorSummary: Buyer agent asking the account-session human to authorize one paid solution run.
latestKnownTruth: The source Request and accepted Artifact can be inspected.
No private run Request or Transaction exists until the spend action is approved and committed.
Payment success would not prove delivery or completion.
example.proposedNextStep: Authorize one idempotency-keyed solution run or cancel before any paid mutation.
agentWillNotDo: I will not spend without account-session approval.
I will not mutate the public source Request.
I will not claim completion from payment reconciliation.

rules: A handoff packet is a renderable explanation and decision request, not a canonical object.
Explicit human approval must still be captured by the governed route, event, review, or payment path that owns the action.
Agents should cite latest durable truth and the next allowed action instead of asking broad approval.
Packets must preserve payment, proof, fulfillment, and completion as separate state labels.
